channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Turns Taiwan Arms Sales Into Bargaining Chip With China
item.description: The president said a potential arms deal for Taiwan was a “very good negotiating chip” in talks with Beijing. His words raise questions about the steadfastness of U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: Patriot missile systems on display during a Taiwanese military exercise in Taipei last year.

item.title: Trump Says a Top ISIS Leader Was Killed in a U.S.-Nigerian Mission
item.description: The leader, whom the State Department designated a terrorist in 2023, had been hiding in Africa, President Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump announced the operation in a late-night post on Truth Social, his social media platform.

item.title: It’s Been a Wild Week in British Politics. What Happens Next?
item.description: Nobody has yet challenged Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership, but his leading rival, Andy Burnham, finally has a route to Downing Street.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Members of the news media congregated outside 10 Downing Street on Thursday, after days of turmoil in the Labour Party over Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s leadership.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Learning Curve’ on China Ends With Conciliation at Summit
item.description: The president has shifted the foundations of American policy toward China, throwing aside the adversarial approach of recent years.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and David E. Sanger
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China arriving for the state banquet in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: How a Drug Cartel Made a Mexican State Its Tool
item.description: Cartel insiders say that in exchange for bribes and political support they were allowed to operate in Sinaloa state with near total freedom.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cartel gunsmiths in a village on the outskirts of Culiacán, in Mexico’s Sinaloa state, in October.

item.title: Inside the Congolese Hotel Where Trump Deported 15 U.S. Migrants
item.description: They were shackled and sent to Kinshasa by the Trump administration. Now they face a dangerous choice: Go back to Latin America or stay in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean, Emiliano Rodríguez Mega, Pranav Baskar and Justin Makangara
item.media:credit: Justin Makangara for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hugo Palencia was deported from the U.S. to Kinshasa, the Democratic Republic of Congo, last month.

item.title: Aging Palestinian Leader Boosting His Son’s Political Rise, Officials Say
item.description: Mahmoud Abbas’s years in power have been dogged by accusations of corruption. Many Palestinians yearn for fresh leadership.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:description: Mahmoud Abbas, president of the Palestinian Authority, center, appearing at a Fatah conference in the Israeli-occupied West Bank city of Ramallah on Thursday.

item.title: Justice Dept. Aims to Use Terrorism Laws to Target Mexican Officials
item.description: In a new tactic, the Justice Department this week instructed federal prosecutors to build criminal drug cases against Mexican officials using terrorism statutes.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño, Alan Feuer and Jack Nicas
item.media:credit: Alejandro Cegarra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Flags at the U.S. border with Mexico in Tijuana last year.

item.title: Executions Surge in Iran Since Cease-Fire, Rights Groups Say
item.description: Many were detained during January’s mass protests, according to a rights group, amid concerns the authorities are trying to intimidate Iranians from returning to the streets.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader who was killed in an airstrike in February, pictured on a billboard in Tehran this month.

item.title: Nvidia’s Future in China Remains Unclear After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: The standoff comes as Chinese firms increasingly turn to domestic chipmakers like Huawei, in a drive to reduce China’s dependence on Western technologies.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.media:description: Jensen Huang, Nvidia’s chief executive, after a welcome ceremony at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Large Ebola Outbreak Is Declared in Congo
item.description: Dozens of deaths and hundreds of infections are suspected, an African agency said. Health experts were alarmed that the outbreak hadn’t been announced sooner.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Ebola treatment center in Mangima, Democratic Republic of Congo, in 2019. Congo and Uganda have seen a string of Ebola outbreaks in recent years.

item.title: Enter the Killer Robots: The Ukrainian Forging the Future of Warfare
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s 35-year-old defense minister, sees futuristic military technology as crucial to his country’s survival.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times

item.title: German Chancellor, After Iran Spat With Trump, Reports ‘Good’ Phone Call
item.description: The call, initiated by Friedrich Merz, came soon after he told young people, “I am a great admirer of America. My admiration is not increasing at the moment.”
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz meeting with people in Würzburg, Germany, on Friday.

item.title: Five Italians Die While Scuba Diving Deep Caves in the Maldives
item.description: The divers, including a marine scientist and her daughter, were part of a research trip and were exploring an underwater cave system when they failed to resurface.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.media:description: Divers and guides returning to shore in Malé, capital of the Maldives, a popular diving destination. The disappearance of five divers this week set off a large search mission, but the authorities have recovered only one body.

item.title: Australian Jews Testify on Experiences of Antisemitism in Bondi Beach Inquiry
item.description: As part of a public inquiry into the massacre at Bondi Beach, dozens have described being targeted for their Jewish identity in their daily lives.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A menorah at a park overlooking Sydney Harbour last December, a few days after the attack at Bondi Beach.

item.title: Trump and Boeing Announce China Will Purchase 200 Planes
item.description: The deal, which Beijing has not commented on, would be a major win for Boeing, which has lost ground to Airbus in one of the world’s largest aviation markets.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher, Qasim Nauman and Niraj Chokshi
item.media:description: Chinese airlines are some of Boeing’s biggest customers.

item.title: In Photos and Video: President Trump Goes to China
item.description: Mr. Trump’s trip, the first U.S. presidential visit to China in almost a decade, was closely watched for signs of whether a trade truce between the countries would hold.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: U.S. President Donald Trump participating in a friendship walk through Zhongnanhai Garden with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

item.title: The Push to Indict Raúl Castro, Cuba’s Former President
item.description: Mr. Castro, Cuba’s former president, could soon face charges in the 1996 killings of four volunteer airmen who were members of a humanitarian group that searched for migrants at sea.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:description: Former President Raúl Castro of Cuba attending a May Day parade in Havana this month.

item.title: With Possible Raúl Castro Indictment, U.S. Eyes Venezuela Playbook
item.description: Amid stepped-up surveillance flights, a visit of the C.I.A. director and an energy embargo, the White House is trying to increase pressure on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Michael Crowley and Frances Robles
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Raúl Castro is the former president of Cuba and younger brother of Fidel Castro.

item.title: Photos: Cuba Plunges Into Darkness as U.S. Cuts Off Oil Supply
item.description: The United States has choked off Cuba’s fuel supply, plunging the already impoverished island into an acute energy crisis.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: People playing dominoes by flashlight as a protest fire burns in the background in Havana on Thursday.

item.title: André Cerdini, Judge in Trial of Nazi Klaus Barbie, Dies at 96
item.description: He calmly presided over the historic 1987 trial in which a French court, for the first time, convicted a Nazi official of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: André Cerdini presiding at the opening of the trial of Klaus Barbie, the former Gestapo chief known as the Butcher of Lyon, on May 11, 1987.

item.title: Kataib Hezbollah: What to Know
item.description: A commander of one of the most-hard line and powerful Iranian proxies in Iraq has been charged with plotting to attack Jewish sites in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A protester holding a Kataib Hezbollah flag in Baghdad, Iraq, in March. The militia was founded after the 2003 U.S. invasion of Iraq.

item.title: Israeli Strike Targeted Top Hamas Leader in Gaza, Officials Say
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the target of an Israeli strike in Gaza City, took over the group’s military wing in Gaza last year. Israeli officials said he was also an architect of the Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas, Jonathan Rosen and Iyad Abuheweila

item.title: Back From China, Trump Faces Decision on Whether to Resume Strikes on Iran
item.description: Top aides have drafted battle plans as peace negotiations have stalled.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump has not yet decided whether to resume military strikes on Iran, but Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told lawmakers that the Pentagon had “a plan to escalate if necessary.”

item.title: Republicans Waited to Challenge Trump on the Iran War. Now It May Be Too Late.
item.description: Having deferred to the president for months, G.O.P. lawmakers missed crucial milestones to try to limit his war powers. That has tied their hands in seeking parameters and exit criteria.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Senator Lisa Murkowski, Republican of Alaska, during a Senate appropriation hearing last month.

item.title: Spotlight on China Adds to Some Chinese Americans’ Fears
item.description: The Trump-Xi summit emphasized stability. But China’s recruitment of foreign agents has fueled suspicion of Chinese Americans.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, on Thursday in Beijing. Two criminal cases in the United States reflect the countries’ competitive and often tense geopolitical relationship.

item.title: Trump Discussed U.S. Arms Sales With Xi ‘In Great Detail’
item.description: The engagement between the president and the Chinese leader may have tested a decades-old U.S. assurance to Taiwan not to consult Beijing on the topic.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China, left, and President Trump discussed American arm sales to Taiwan in “great detail,” according to Mr. Trump.

item.title: Xi Pitches His Vision for Avoiding Conflict With the U.S.
item.description: Having fought the Trump administration to a draw, China’s Xi Jinping is proposing “constructive strategic stability,” aimed at drawing lines he thinks the U.S. should not cross.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Lily Kuo
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China welcomed President Trump in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: From Air Force One, Trump writes of ballrooms, a statue and TikTok.
item.description: In social media posts after the summit in Beijing, President Trump boasted about the ongoing construction of his ballroom and his TikTok reach.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: President Trump waving on the stairs of Air Force One at the end of his trip in China on Friday.

item.title: Despite Tensions, China Greets Trump With Pomp and Pageantry
item.description: There was no major breakthrough in Beijing, but China pulled out all the stops for President Trump, with extravagant ceremonies and a state banquet.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Rows of cheering children welcomed President Trump at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: ‘For Example, Putin’: How Xi Used a Private Garden Walk to Charm Trump
item.description: Watch an exchange between the two men in which the Chinese leader sought to signal exclusivity and name-dropped Russia’s president.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo, Jiawei Wang and Pei-Lin Wu

item.title: What to Know About Day 2 of Trump and Xi’s Beijing Summit
item.description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, emphasized stability but announced no major breakthroughs on points of contention like trade, Taiwan and the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, meeting in Zhongnanhai, a compound used by the Communist Party, in Beijing on Friday.

item.title: Trump Touts ‘Fantastic Trade Deals’ With China, but Details Are Scarce
item.description: The president left Beijing following a summit with China, during which the two countries sought to stabilize their economic and political relations.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: President Trump before departing China in Beijing on Friday.

item.title: Delivering Mail on Ukraine’s Front Line
item.description: Larysa Navrotska risks her life to deliver mail, retirement checks and medicine to remote Ukrainian communities under the constant threat of Russian drones from the nearby front line. Her service has become even more crucial than it was before the war.
item.dc:creator: Michael Anthony Adams, Whitney Shefte, Dzvinka Pinchuk, Jon Hazell and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Sanctioned by China, Rubio Enjoys a Trip to Beijing
item.description: There has been rampant speculation online about whether the Chinese government changed the transliteration of Marco Rubio’s name to overlook sanctions. But that theory is wrong.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Marco Rubio at the state banquet in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. and China Will Start Discussing A.I. Safety, Bessent Says
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent did not say when these talks would happen. There are fears in the United States and China about the threats from A.I., but neither side is willing to slow down its development.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent during the welcoming ceremony for President Trump in Beijing, on Thursday.

item.title: What is Zhongnanhai, the Secretive Beijing Compound Where Trump Met Xi?
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meeting with President Trump on Friday at Zhongnanhai, a heavily guarded Beijing compound where top Chinese officials live and work.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Joy Dong
item.media:description: Security outside Zhongnanhai, the official residence of China’s leadership, on Wednesday.

item.title: China Starting to Fulfill ‘Promises,’ U.S. Trade Representative Says
item.description: As President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, meet, the United States said China has promised to expand its purchases of farm goods and airplanes.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, left, during a state dinner with President Trump and China’s President Xi Jinping at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, on Thursday,

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Ana Swanson, David Pierson and Anton Troianovski

item.title: 16 More People in the U.S. Are Being Monitored for Hantavirus, C.D.C. Says
item.description: They were passengers on a plane to Johannesburg with an infected Dutch woman who later died.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A person in a biohazard suit at the airport in Omaha, Neb., as passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius arrived on Monday.

item.title: Glitz and Boycotts at Eurovision
item.description: The song contest has camp, glitter and pyrotechnics. And, this year, protests over Israel’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Cuba Says It Has Run Out of Oil
item.description: A long-running energy crisis in the island nation reached a breaking point this week, when government officials said they had run out of reserves.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gas station in Havana this month. In January, the Trump administration cut off oil shipments from Venezuela, which had been supplying a large amount of Cuba’s oil.

item.title: Trump’s and Xi’s Body Language at the Summit Mirrored Their Styles
item.description: Although at odds over issues like trade and Taiwan, the U.S. and Chinese leaders met in Beijing with a show of friendly gestures.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas

item.title: A Top U.S. Commander Dismisses Reports of Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said a strike on an Iranian school may have been the only civilian casualty incident of the war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Admiral Cooper told the lawmakers that his staff had been proactive in trying to prevent civilian deaths. “Our staff specifically warned the Iranian people more than 100 times about the threat of them being used as human shields,” he said.

item.title: Nigel Farage, Leader of Reform UK, Says £5 Million Gift Was ‘Reward’ for Brexit
item.description: Nigel Farage, the leader of the populist right-wing party, had previously said the money was for personal security.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage during a campaign event in London in April.

item.title: Iran Lets Chinese Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions After Vessel Seizure
item.description: Iranian news agencies said some Chinese ships were being allowed through the strait, following diplomatic outreach to Iran from Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz early this month.

item.title: Trump Was Flattering, Xi Was Resolute. The Difference Spoke Volumes.
item.description: In contrast to his rhetoric about China at home, President Trump spoke in conciliatory terms with Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.media:description: President Trump with President Xi Jinping of China in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Spies, Sanctions, Cyberattacks: China and the U.S. Clash Behind the Scenes
item.description: After months of avoiding confrontation, the Trump administration has taken recent steps to call out China on Iran, artificial intelligence and spying.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Dustin Volz
item.media:description: President Trump met with President Xi Jinping of China on Thursday after a flurry of actions from his administration in recent weeks against China.

item.title: Hantavirus Doesn’t Spread Easily, but Officials May Be Downplaying Risks
item.description: The virus is clearly far less contagious than the coronavirus, scientists agree, but they have found cases where it spread among people without direct contact.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Passengers from the MV Hondius evacuated from the cruise ship this week in Tenerife, Spain.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and U.A.E. Carried Out Secret Attacks in Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The Gulf Arab states have been grappling with how to deter Iran after the war made clear the limits of American security guarantees.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran, the capital of Iran, last week.

item.title: Xi Warned Trump About the ‘Thucydides Trap’ for U.S. and China. What Is It?
item.description: China’s leader reached for Greek history to warn the United States of what can happen when a rising power meets an incumbent one.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:credit: Eye Ubiquitous/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A statue of Thucydides, the Athenian general and historian, outside the parliament building in Vienna.

item.title: Trump’s Push to Keep Coal Plants Open Is Costing Hundreds of Millions
item.description: Costs have been mounting in the year since the Trump administration began directing aging coal plants to stay open.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.media:credit: Jim West/UCG/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The J.H. Campbell coal-fired power plant in West Olive, Mich., one of five coal plants in the United States that President Trump ordered to remain open. The plant has reported $180 million in expenses associated with running the facility since last May.

item.title: Who Is Wes Streeting, the Cabinet Minister Who Just Resigned From Keir Starmer’s Government?
item.description: Mr. Streeting attributes his ability to connect with a range of voters to his background. He was the first in his family to graduate from college and his grandfather went to prison for armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Wes Streeting has prided himself on a background that he said enables him to relate to many voters.

item.title: Trump Might Welcome Chinese Investment, but America Is Wary
item.description: A pledge for more Chinese investment could face backlash given longstanding national security concerns in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.media:description: President Trump on Thursday in Beijing. There has been speculation that Mr. Trump and his advisers are seeking to secure a major investment pledge from China on the trip.

item.title: Angela Rayner, Former U.K. Deputy Prime Minister, Cleared of Wrongdoing Over Taxes
item.description: Angela Rayner, who resigned last year after paying the wrong rate of tax on an apartment purchase, said an investigation found she had not done so deliberately.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Angela Rayner last year. She has been seen as a potential successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Who Attended the U.S.-China State Dinner in Beijing?
item.description: Guests included top U.S. and Chinese officials and the chief executives of several major American companies.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio attended the state dinner at the Great Hall of the People.

item.title: Who Owns These Artworks? Musée d’Orsay Hopes Visitors Can Help Find Out.
item.description: A new room in the Musée d’Orsay’s permanent display includes 13 pieces that were recovered from Germany and Austria after World War II and whose provenance is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Ségolène Le Stradic

item.title: Over 100 People Killed in India’s Uttar Pradesh as Storms Wreak Destruction
item.description: The deaths were scattered across a swath of India’s most populous state, Uttar Pradesh, where many homes were destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Hari Kumar and Nazaneen Ghaffar
item.media:description: Storm damage in Prayagraj, a city in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh, on Wednesday.

item.title: Where Are the Women on Trump’s Visit to China?
item.description: Nearly all the business leaders and officials accompanying President Trump in China are men. There are few women on the Chinese side, too.
item.dc:creator: Emily McGarvey
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China greeting the U.S. delegation at the Great Hall of the People on Thursday.

item.title: What People in China Say of Trump
item.description: Residents in four Chinese cities described a mixture of amusement and anger, blaming U.S. tensions for a slowing economy and rising fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: President Trump at the state banquet in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Invites Xi to White House During State Dinner in Beijing
item.description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China emphasized cooperation in their remarks after walking into the Great Hall of the People side by side.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Luke Broadwater
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, emphasized their cooperation on Thursday.

item.title: Wes Streeting Resigns and Calls for Leadership Contest to Replace Starmer
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer now appears set to face at least two possible challengers: his health minister, who resigned Thursday, and the popular mayor of Manchester.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Wes Streeting resigned from his job as health minister on Thursday and called for a leadership contest against Mr. Starmer.
